recipe.title: Perfect roast beef
recipe.description: This Jamie Oliver roast beef recipe is perfect for a Sunday lunch. Let the beef rest for half an hour, then slice very thinly for super-tender roast beef.

ingredients.name: mixed fresh herbs
ingredients.note: such as thyme, rosemary, bay, sage

steps.descr: Remove the beef from the fridge 30 minutes before you...
steps.instructions: Remove the beef from the fridge 30 minutes before you want to cook it, to let it come up to room temperature.

on_enter.say: Remove the beef from the fridge 30 minutes before you want to cook it, to let it come up to room temperature.

steps.descr: Wash and roughly chop the vegetables – there’s no need...
steps.instructions: Wash and roughly chop the vegetables – there’s no need to peel them. Break the garlic bulb into cloves, leaving them unpeeled.

on_enter.say: Wash and roughly chop the vegetables – there’s no need to peel them. Break the garlic bulb into cloves, leaving them unpeeled.

steps.descr: Pile all the veg, garlic and herbs into the middle of a...
steps.instructions: Pile all the veg, garlic and herbs into the middle of a large roasting tray and drizzle with oil.

on_enter.say: Pile all the veg, garlic and herbs into the middle of a large roasting tray and drizzle with oil.

steps.descr: Drizzle the beef with oil and season well with sea salt...
steps.instructions: Drizzle the beef with oil and season well with sea salt and black pepper, then rub all over the meat. Place the beef on top of the vegetables.

on_enter.say: Drizzle the beef with oil and season well with sea salt and black pepper, then rub all over the meat. Place the beef on top of the vegetables.

steps.descr: Place the tray in the oven, then turn the heat down...
steps.instructions: Place the tray in the oven, then turn the heat down immediately to 200°C/400°F/gas 6 and cook for 1 hour for medium beef. If you prefer it medium-rare, take it out 5 to 10 minutes earlier. For well done, leave it in for another 10 to 15 minutes.

on_enter.say: Place the tray in the oven, then turn the heat down immediately to 200°C/400°F/gas 6 and cook for 1 hour for medium beef. If you prefer it medium-rare, take it out 5 to 10 minutes earlier. For well done, leave it in for another 10 to 15 minutes.

steps.descr: If you’re doing roast potatoes and veggies, this is the...
steps.instructions: If you’re doing roast potatoes and veggies, this is the time to crack on with them – get them into the oven for the last 45 minutes of cooking.

on_enter.say: If you’re doing roast potatoes and veggies, this is the time to crack on with them – get them into the oven for the last 45 minutes of cooking.

steps.descr: Baste the beef halfway through cooking and if the veg...
steps.instructions: Baste the beef halfway through cooking and if the veg look dry, add a splash of water to the tray to stop them from burning.

on_enter.say: Baste the beef halfway through cooking and if the veg look dry, add a splash of water to the tray to stop them from burning.

steps.descr: When the beef is cooked to your liking, take the tray...
steps.instructions: When the beef is cooked to your liking, take the tray out of the oven and transfer the beef to a board to rest for 15 minutes or so. Cover it with a layer of tin foil and a tea towel and leave aside while you make your gravy, horseradish sauce and Yorkshire puddings.

on_enter.say: When the beef is cooked to your liking, take the tray out of the oven and transfer the beef to a board to rest for 15 minutes or so. Cover it with a layer of tin foil and a tea towel and leave aside while you make your gravy, horseradish sauce and Yorkshire puddings.
